title: C string representation deterministic evidence bank

items.title: C string representation: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? Every char array is automatically null-terminated and safe to pass to strlen or printf with %s.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Before using string functions, prove that a null terminator occurs within the valid array bounds and keep capacity for it.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Before using string functions, prove that a null terminator occurs within the valid array bounds and keep capacity for it.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

items.title: C string representation: complete the language rule
items.prompt: Complete the missing C checkpoint in the source-aligned correction. Use the exact token or phrase required by this draft.
hints: Use the checkpoint that distinguishes the valid rule from this misconception: Every char array is automatically null-terminated and safe to pass to strlen or printf with %s.

feedback.correct: Correct. The required checkpoint is 'null terminator'.
feedback.incorrect: Recheck the exact language rule and its boundary. Before using string functions, prove that a null terminator occurs within the valid array bounds and keep capacity for it.

items.title: C string representation: executable application
items.prompt: Read at most 40 non-newline characters with fgets, remove only the trailing newline when present, and compute the string length by scanning to the null terminator.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: bounded character array and terminating null character

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises bounded character array and terminating null character under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
